title: podophyllum peltatum f. callicarpum
intro: Podophyllum peltatum f. callicarpum, also known as American mayapple or wild mandrake, is a plant species that belongs to the barberry family and is native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of the plant are attributed to the presence of podophyllotoxin, a lignan that has been shown to have antitumor and antimicrobial activities. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, salve, or tea, and is often used in herbal medicine to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
